content: Preclinical success criteria include: demonstrated mechanism of action at intended target, acceptable safety margin (typically 10-100x exposure over anticipated human dose), favorable pharmacokinetic profile (oral bioavailability >20%, half-life supporting dosing frequency), no genotoxicity or carcinogenicity signals, and scalable synthesis. Failure modes include poor solubility, metabolic instability, off-target toxicity, lack of efficacy in disease models, or inability to achieve therapeutic concentrations. FDA requires GLP-compliant toxicology studies before IND submission.
